title: Death and funeral of Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei
summary: Iran held an elaborate multi-day state funeral and public ceremonies following the death of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in a February 2026 airstrike on Tehran, drawing massive crowds across multiple cities.

events.chapter_summary: Iran held a six-day, five-city state funeral for Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei following his death on 28 February 2026 in a US-Israeli airstrike on a Tehran government residence, with ceremonies concluding at his burial in Mashhad near the Imam Reza shrine.

A major funeral procession for Khamenei occurred in Tehran on July 7, 2026, with state media estimating millions of mourners along a 10-kilometer route, and took place amid ongoing Iran-U.S. negotiations following the February 28 war.

Iran conducted Khamenei's burial on July 10, 2026, at the Shrine of Imam Reza in Mashhad, concluding a week-long funeral procession through multiple Iranian and Iraqi cities, while US-Iran tensions threatened a peace agreement negotiated three weeks prior.
